title: Codex blocking/non-blocking classification undefined — merge gate is fragile
body: Codex finding classification into blocking/non-blocking is the gate that determines whether auto-merge fires — it cannot be left undefined. If delegated to Codex's own uncontrolled output format, the gate is fragile.

title: Concurrent invocation unprotected — two runs corrupt git state and artifacts
body: Nothing prevents two `/autorun` invocations against the same queue simultaneously. The result is competing git operations and corrupted artifacts.

title: Test suite undefined — auto-merge gate has no implementation path
body: "Tests pass" gates auto-merge (AC #3) and 3-retry rollback (AC #5). No test runner, invocation command, exit-code contract, or handling for 'no test suite' is defined.

title: osascript silently fails in locked-screen / LaunchDaemon context
body: `osascript` requires an active GUI session. A launchd `LaunchDaemon` (root) or screen-locked session will silently fail osascript — overnight desktop banner is unreliable.

title: Branch strategy undefined — feature branches and PR target unspecified
body: `/build` writes code, but the spec never says: who creates the feature branch, what's it named, what happens if two queue items touch the same files, and what branch does `gh pr create` target.

title: Pre-build SHA capture undefined — rollback has no implementation contract
body: `git reset --hard to pre-build SHA` appears as the rollback mechanism, but the data model has no field for this SHA and the scripts have no contract for capturing it.

title: Credential handling in launchd: tokens unavailable in minimal environment
body: The pipeline invokes `gh`, `claude -p`, Codex, and potentially a webhook. A launchd job runs in a minimal environment with no `.zshrc`, no keychain auto-unlock, no `SSH_AUTH_SOCK`. The first overnight run will silently fail without explicit credential wiring.

title: Stage 2 context handoff mechanism unspecified
body: "plan (sees stage 1 findings as context)" is undefined. Is `review-findings.md` appended to the plan prompt? Passed via file path argument? The plan wrapper script needs a contract.

title: "One autonomous fix attempt" has no exit condition — loop risk
body: The autonomy directive says 'make the call.' The spec also says 'one autonomous fix attempt' and then halts. If the fix produces a new blocking finding, does the directive tell the agent to try again? No tiebreaker is stated.

title: Parallel claude -p rate limits: 7 concurrent processes may produce silent failur
body: 7 concurrent `claude -p` processes (6 spec-reviewers + risk-analysis) with no rate-limit handling. API per-minute token limits may produce silent partial outputs that the threshold gate misreads.

title: PR provenance block format unspecified — AC #1 has no template
body: AC #1 asserts a 'provenance block' in the PR body with no template or required fields defined. Builders will produce inconsistent output.

title: claude -p invocation model is architecturally inverted
body: The spec shows `claude -p "$(cat scripts/autorun/run.sh)"` — but a shell script is not a prompt. Either the orchestrator is a shell script that *calls* `claude -p`, or Claude *generates* the orchestration. These are two different architectures.

title: Notification event triggers and payload unspecified
body: `webhook_url` and `mail_to` are config fields but the payload schema, retry behavior on delivery failure, and which events trigger a notification (halt? complete? each stage failure?) are not described.

title: /spec --auto flag is an undeclared dependency
body: AC #7 requires `/spec --auto` but this flag doesn't exist in the current `/spec` command. Building `/autorun` before building `--auto` mode creates a broken acceptance criterion.

title: "Fatal" severity undefined — spec-review threshold gate is unimplementable
body: `spec_review_fatal_threshold: 2` is configurable, but 'fatal' has no definition. Two engineers will implement this differently — one might parse output for the word 'fatal,' another might implement a severity enum.

title: Build waves undefined — Stage 4 control flow cannot be implemented
body: Stage 4 references 'build waves,' 'execute wave,' and 'check kill-switch' without explaining what a wave is, how many there are, how they're sized, or what determines wave boundaries. `scripts/autorun/build.sh` cannot be written without this.
